item.title: Are We Building the Infrastructure of Unfreedom?
item.description: [Climate & Environment] Keyword: climate change — How many big issues should our civilization be concerned about today? Whatever the number, most will agree that there are now too many. Concern for the environment and climate change has been around for decades. Europe over the past two months has endured record-breaking heat waves. The liberating joy associated with late spring sunshine accompanied… Continue reading Are We Building the Infrastructure of Unfreedom? The post Are We Building the Infrastructure of Unfreedom? appeared first on

item.title: EV myths muddy the road to electrification
item.description: [Open Source Software] Keyword: FOSS — Claims that electric vehicle batteries are tainted by exploitative mineral supply chains are discrediting non-fossil cars as electrified road transport booms.

item.title: Mageia 10 released
item.description: [Open Source Software] Keyword: Linux kernel — Mageia 10 has been released with the 6.18 Linux kernel, DNF 5.4.0, RPM 4.20.1, and an increase in hardware requirements for x86 32-bit systems; users now need a CPU with SSE2 features. See the release notes for a full list of updates, and the errata page for known problems.

item.title: Europe heatwave shows need to reject climate denial ‘lies’, says EU green chief
item.description: [Open Source Software] Keyword: FOSS — Teresa Ribera blames ‘ideologically driven’ falsehoods, driven by those with vested interests in fossil fuels, for attacks on green policyThe heatwave wreaking chaos across Europe is a “dramatic warning” to reject climate naysayers, a European Commission vice-president says.Teresa Ribera, executive vice-president for a clean, just and competitive transition, lambasted those who listened to the “vested interests” of the fossil fuel industry rather than scientists and their own citizens. Continue

item.title: Lebanon’s displaced begin to return home, but some have nowhere to go
item.description: [Housing & Gentrification] Keyword: displacement — Thousands in Lebanon attempt to return home, yet rampant destruction leaves many trapped in displacement centres.

item.title: US military races to vaccinate new recruits before flu shots expire
item.description: [Texas & San Antonio Local] Keyword: San Antonio — Move comes amid growing outbreak in Texas and after two-month halt on mandatory shotsThe US military is racing to vaccinate new recruits after a two-month halt on mandatory flu shots – but it’s a temporary reprieve, as the shots will soon expire and new doses will not be available for months.Officials will need to lean on other prevention measures to contain the growing flu outbreak at Lackland air force base in San Antonio, Texas, experts say. Continue reading...

item.title: The New Five Forces, Part 3: Society Expects Accountability
item.description: [AI & Automation] Keyword: LLM — [This is the third part of a five-part series adapted from Dr. Noa Gafni’s report, The New Five Forces: A Blueprint for Business in an Uncertain World. To read more, see Parts 1 and 2 here.] In the second installment of this series on the New Five Forces, we examined how geopolitics has become a… Continue reading The New Five Forces, Part 3: Society Expects Accountability The post The New Five Forces, Part 3: Society Expects Accountability appeared first on Fair Observer.

item.title: Camera traps reveal the true culprit behind crop damage in Honduras
item.description: [Ecology & Conservation] Keyword: wildlife conservation — A new study from the Honduran Mosquitia shows how simple, noninvasive technology can help solve one of the most common challenges in wildlife conservation: identifying the species actually responsible for crop damage. The full detailed results have been published in Neotropical Biology and Conservation.

item.title: Universities Join Hands to Enhance Agroforestry Research for Mitigating Climate Change
item.description: [Climate & Environment] Keyword: climate change — A team of universities, led by Addis Ababa University, has joined forces to implement a four-year Intra-Africa academic mobility project aimed at strengthening agroforestry research and education for climate change mitigation. The project, dubbed Strengthening Agroforestry Research and Education for Climate Change Mitigation in Africa (SERA), brings together JKUAT (Kenya) and Addis Ababa University (Ethiopia) […]

item.title: Offshore Oil and Gas Rush Threatens Whale Corridors and Coral Reefs
item.description: [Open Source Software] Keyword: FOSS — From coral reefs in Kenya to Caribbean seagrass meadows and whale migration corridors in the Arctic, a surge in offshore oil, gas and liquefied natural gas development is spreading into some of the world’s most ecologically important marine habitats, according to a new analysis. In many cases, researchers from Earth Insight—a nonprofit that maps fossil […]

item.title: Five Americans die every hour from toxic vehicle emissions, study finds
item.description: [Open Source Software] Keyword: FOSS — Research suggests 41,800 premature US deaths in 2024 were attributable to road pollutionRoughly five Americans die every hour due to exposure to toxic road vehicle pollution, a new study has found.It’s the latest warning showing fossil-fueled transit is a major driver of mortality. Continue reading...

item.title: Supreme court nears the end of its term with cases about Donald Trump’s power to be decided – US politics live
item.description: [Open Source Software] Keyword: FOSS — Court expected to hand down decisions on several outstanding cases, wrapping up term that has focussed on Trump’s expansive claims of presidential powerSign up for the Breaking News US emailThe Trump administration is attempting to shrink public comment periods for fossil fuel leasing on federal land while shifting the financial risks of cleanup to taxpayers and allowing for more planet-warming emissions. It’s part of a broader effort to dismantle public input processes and save polluting compan

item.title: Germany: At least 5 killed in shooting in Stade
item.description: [Immigration & Border] Keyword: ICE operation — A large police operation is underway in the northern German city after several shots were fired. Several people are reported dead and authorities say it is a "dynamic situation."

item.title: Q&A: NEISD’s newest leader talks challenges, AI and changing public education landscape
item.description: [Texas & San Antonio Local] Keyword: San Antonio — The Report sat down with NEISD's new superintendent to talk about challenges facing public education amid a wave of changes. Q&A: NEISD’s newest leader talks challenges, AI and changing public education landscape was first posted on June 29, 2026 at 5:00 am.©2021 "San Antonio Report". Use of this feed is for personal non-commercial use only. If you are not reading this article in your feed reader, then the site is guilty of copyright infringement. Please contact me at he

item.title: Australia politics live: Andrew Hastie claims multiculturalism a ‘loaded political term’; Indigenous words stripped from Queensland state electorates
item.description: [Indigenous News & Tribal Sovereignty] Keyword: Indigenous — Follow today’s news liveGet our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcastRoyal commission hearings resume todayThe next round of hearings for the antisemitism royal commission begins today.Regretfully, we’re here until Thursday night with Parliament and the game is at 4am Saturday morning. So I’ll be watching on the TV. And like I’ve said before, sometimes I’m the minister for sport and it’s important I’m at major events because there’s all kinds of elements and stakeholders that are t

item.title: The order of species loss alters how grasslands maintain stability, study finds
item.description: [Climate & Environment] Keyword: climate change — Grasslands account for roughly 40% of terrestrial ecosystems and are paramount to global food security. Wild grasslands provide food for livestock and habitat for pollinators and act as a carbon sink in the era of climate change. Maintaining the health of native grasslands is a critical component of protecting the human food supply and sustaining biodiversity in the face of rising global temperatures and changes in weather patterns.

item.title: First dinosaur bone from Antarctica found in a drawer
item.description: [Open Source Software] Keyword: FOSS — The fossil, collected in Antarctica in 1985, is part of the tail of a beast called a Titanosaur.

item.title: ‘Get your dirty hands off our kids’: fossil fuel industry’s presence in schools and sports clubs sparks concern
item.description: [Open Source Software] Keyword: FOSS — Greens and David Pocock accuse industry of using programs and sponsorships to ‘build social licence’, push for parliamentary inquiryFollow our Australia news live blog for latest updatesGet our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcastThe Greens and David Pocock have backed calls for a parliamentary inquiry into the alleged “petro-grooming” of children after an investigation exposed the extent of the fossil fuel sector’s presence in schools, sports clubs and other educational instituti

item.title: One person a week in England dies with undiagnosed TB, study finds
item.description: [Cannabis, Hemp & Drug Policy] Keyword: THCA — British-born, older men among those most likely to have disease found only postmortem, say researchersOne person a week dies with undiagnosed and therefore untreated tuberculosis in England, a study has found.British-born, older men were among those most likely to have TB diagnosed only after death, researchers said, suggesting healthcare workers could be overlooking the possibility of the disease in these patients. Continue reading...

item.title: India’s Exam System Under Fire as NEET and CBSE Controversies Grow
item.description: [ALPR & Flock Cameras] Keyword: ALPR — Repeated leaks, technical failures and malpractice allegations have shaken trust in India’s examination system and drawn students’ anger. Experts hold the National Testing Agency (NTA) responsible. They argue that the NTA was established as a society, which, under the Indian Constitution, refers to a voluntary association created for charitable or nonprofit purposes. This organization has… Continue reading India’s Exam System Under Fire as NEET and CBSE Controversies Grow The post India’s

item.title: More License Plate Reader Mission Creep: School Residency Verification, Background Checks, and Noise Complaints
item.description: [ALPR & Flock Cameras] Keyword: Flock Safety — An EFF analysis of millions of searches of Flock Safety automated license plate reader (ALPR) data by police has uncovered a troubling pattern: in the absence of a warrant requirement to search ALPR databases, law enforcement agencies have moved beyond specific investigations to use these surveillance networks for virtually any whim. Our findings suggest that the absence of a warrant requirement has fostered a culture of unrestricted access to sensitive location data, allowing agencies to levera

item.title: 🔊 Mass Surveillance for… Loud Music? | EFFector 38.11
item.description: [ALPR & Flock Cameras] Keyword: license plate reader — Across the country, surveillance companies have spun a vast web of tens of thousands of license plate cameras. The people selling this tech want you to believe that it's for your safety, but how are authorities really using automated license plate readers (ALPR)? In this week's EFFector newsletter, we're looking at how these powerful surveillance networks have become universal people-trackers used for noise complaints and other low-level investigations. JOIN OUR NEWSLETTER For over 35 years, EFF

item.title: Are Your Local Police Using Flock Safety ALPRs to Scan for Immigrants?
item.description: [ALPR & Flock Cameras] Keyword: Flock Safety — When a car passes an automated license plate reader (ALPR), its plate is captured and instantly compared against a list of vehicles that police are actively looking for or that police have identified for real-time surveillance. These are called “hotlists,” and EFF has learned that one used by agencies across the country targets immigrants on behalf of Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE). Agencies using Flock Safety ALPR systems commonly allow the plates their cameras collect to be compare

item.title: He saw an abandoned trailer. Then, he uncovered a surveillance network on California's border
item.description: [ALPR & Flock Cameras] Keyword: license plate reader — Southern California residents are noticing new license plate readers that appear to be operated by the Border Patrol. Some have had confusing encounters with agents.

item.description: [SAPD Calls for Service] Keyword: Assault In Progress — Incident #SAPD-2026-0878176 | EAST Division | 6/28/2026 7:54:38 PM

item.title: Over one million children referred for mental healthcare - with anxiety the main reason
item.description: [Cannabis, Hemp & Drug Policy] Keyword: THCA — Demand is soaring beyond capacity, meaning children in England wait years for help with various conditions.

item.title: How Rhode Island’s Labor Movement Made Its Weight Felt in Politics
item.description:
[Labor & Worker Rights] Keyword: labor movement — You must log in or register to post a comment.
April 22, 2026 / Paul Prescod
Enlarge or shrink text
login or register to comment
Over the last decade, unions in Rhode Island have shifted the political fortunes of working people in the state. Photo: Ryan T. Conaty Photography. Over the last decade Rhode Island has been a hotbed of progressive, pro-worker legislation. But it wasn’t always this way. It took years of proactive organizing by the labor movement on legislative and elector

item.title: From Cell to Cell, Jailed Minnesota Unionists Sang of Freedom
item.description:
[Labor & Worker Rights] Keyword: labor notes — EnglishEspañolYou must log in or register to post a comment.
June 17, 2026 / Emmett Doyle
Enlarge or shrink text
login or register to comment
Post a Comment Yesterday the federal government arrested and indicted 15 people, many of them union activists, on conspiracy charges over the protests and massive strike against ICE in Minnesota last winter. Two of those indicted had just returned from the Labor Notes Conference, where Emmett Doyle’s prize-winning song “Hold the Line” w

item.title: Virginia Workers to Governor: Don't Water Down Our Bargaining Rights!
item.description:
[Worker Cooperatives & Solidarity Economy] Keyword: collective bargaining — You must log in or register to post a comment.
April 14, 2026 / Lee Abbott
Enlarge or shrink text
login or register to comment
Members of the United Campus Workers of Virginia marched to the state’s capitol in Richmond, Virginia. They are still trying to get college instructional staff included in the new public worker bargaining law. Photo: UCW-VA After a years-long campaign by unions, Virginia’s General Assembly passed legislation to extend collective bargaining rights to nearly

item.title: Texas Organizers Go to Tokyo to Tell Japan and its Banks: Stop Funding U.S. LNG
item.description: [Open Source Software] Keyword: FOSS — Seeking fossil fuel superpower status, Japan and its megabanks have spent at least $38B on natural gas projects in Texas alone.

item.title: 2025's Hill County Floods Are Our State's New 'Whiplash Weather.' There Will be More.
item.description: [Open Source Software] Keyword: FOSS — Rapid shifts from extreme drought to deadly floods is a feature, not a bug, in our new fossil-fuels modified climate. Here's what you need to know.

item.title: Dozens of Countries Just Met to Chart a Path off Fossil Fuels—No Lobbyists Invited
item.description: [Open Source Software] Keyword: FOSS — At the first international conference of its kind, almost 60 nations shared ideas, exchanged best practices, and discussed the hurdles they face in their effort to end their dependence on oil, gas, and coal.

item.title: [$] Reports from OSPM 2026, day three
item.description: [Open Source Software] Keyword: Linux kernel — The Power Management and Scheduling in the Linux Kernel Summit, which still goes by the historical acronym OSPM, was held in Cambridge, UK, in mid-April. As has become traditional, the presenters at that event have since written summaries of their sessions, and this work has kindly been made available to LWN for publication. The third day's sessions covered a wide range of topics, including GPU affinity, profile-guided scheduling, paravirtualization scheduling, quality of service, and more.

item.title: They Asked for Help. Instead, They Died in Solitary.
item.description: [Police & Criminal Justice] Keyword: solitary confinement — An investigation found there were at least 47 suicides in solitary confinement in Mississippi, where cries for mental health care were met with isolation and punishment.

item.title: They Spent Years in Solitary Confinement in Mississippi Despite Suicide Risk
item.description: [Police & Criminal Justice] Keyword: solitary confinement — For decades, MDOC has regularly held incarcerated people in solitary confinement for weeks, months — and in some cases — years at a time.

item.title: Libraries Not Doing Pride Displays Say They ‘Shouldn’t Be Judged’
item.description: [Government Accountability] Keyword: public records — Public records show dozens of libraries have self-censored to avoid attracting negative attention.

item.title: Yonder Radio: Rural Dialysis Care, Appalachian Ballad Swapping, and Black Footed Ferret Restoration
item.description: [Diabetes & Kidney Disease] Keyword: dialysis — Tune in for conversations about rural dialysis care, the tradition of Appalachian ballad swapping, and restoring the Black Footed Ferret. Plus, we celebrate the migration of Sandhill cranes in Colorado's San Luis Valley, and hear music from producer and musician Bill Palmer. The post Yonder Radio: Rural Dialysis Care, Appalachian Ballad Swapping, and Black Footed Ferret Restoration appeared first on The Daily Yonder.

item.title: People With Type 1 Diabetes Denied Basic Medical Care in Pennsylvania Prisons, Lawsuit Alleges
item.description: [Diabetes & Kidney Disease] Keyword: type 1 diabetes — A lawsuit filed by Disability Rights Pennsylvania says that the state’s prison system restricts access to insulin pumps and glucose monitors, putting people with Type 1 diabetes at risk of death or serious injuries. The post People With Type 1 Diabetes Denied Basic Medical Care in Pennsylvania Prisons, Lawsuit Alleges appeared first on The Appeal.

item.title: Border Walls & ‘Murder Buoys’: Emerging Risks Facing One of the Americas' Great Rivers—and Those Who Call Her Home
item.description: [Immigration & Border] Keyword: border wall — ‘Every state a border state’ has shown the U.S. interior MAGA’s anti-immigrant furor, but on the actual southern boundary, border residents—and the Rio Grande they share—are bearing the fuller price of ever expanding militarization.

item.title: Yonder Radio: Data Center Regulation, A Border Wall in Big Bend, and Rural Assembly Everywhere
item.description: [Immigration & Border] Keyword: border wall — Tune in for conversations about data center regulation and plans to build the border wall through the Big Bend region of West Texas. Plus, an introduction to Rural Assembly Everywhere, efforts to fight coastal erosion in Washington's cranberry country, and music from heavy metal guitarist Gabe Mangold. The post Yonder Radio: Data Center Regulation, A Border Wall in Big Bend, and Rural Assembly Everywhere appeared first on The Daily Yonder.

item.title: Unsatisfied With Record Profits, Private Prison Investors Want ICE to Escalate
item.description: [Immigration & Border] Keyword: immigration detention — Investors on CoreCivic and GEO Group’s quarterly earnings calls expressed frustration that ICE’s record immigration detention numbers aren’t high enough. The post Unsatisfied With Record Profits, Private Prison Investors Want ICE to Escalate appeared first on The Appeal.

item.title: 'Every Bombed Village'— Deceleration's Spring 2026 Creative Review
item.description: [Texas & San Antonio Local] Keyword: San Antonio — Writers and artists from San Antonio to Quezon City respond to James Baldwin's historic words about 'every bombed village' being our own hometowns as we witness US-backed bombs fall on Iran, Gaza, Lebanon, Venezuela, and so many other places.

item.title: 'Is This Our Future?' San Antonio Data Center Hearing Smacks of Trolling by the State
item.description: [Texas & San Antonio Local] Keyword: San Antonio — Plus: Massive global data center study, Trump's fake oversight EO, accelerating surveillance of data center opposition, action opportunities, y más.

item.title: Data Centers Bringing Major New Pollution Sources to San Antonio’s West Side
item.description: [Texas & San Antonio Local] Keyword: San Antonio — Frustration marked public hearing for Vantage Data Center’s TX2 expansion plans at Highway 151 and Wiseman.

item.title: San Antonio Data Center Meeting to Explore Possible Regulation Through Zoning
item.description: [Texas & San Antonio Local] Keyword: San Antonio — City of San Antonio's Development Services hosts its second meeting of a data center Stakeholder Discussion Group dedicated to industrial power use.

item.title: CPS Energy Board Unravels its Climate Commitments as Data Center Power Demands Grow
item.description: [Texas & San Antonio Local] Keyword: San Antonio — Climate emissions have been growing in San Antonio, despite Council and utility pledges to drive them down. Monday's CPS Board action all but guarantees future targets will remain out of reach as Texas cooks.

item.title: Pearl developer Oxbow planning 9-story mixed-use building on East Josephine
item.description: [Texas & San Antonio Local] Keyword: San Antonio — The Pearl’s development company is moving forward with plans to build a 9-story mixed-use structure, which would include 250-275 market-rate apartments, on a full city block bound by East Josephine, Isleta, East Grayson and the river. The post Pearl developer Oxbow planning 9-story mixed-use building on East Josephine appeared first on San Antonio Heron.

item.title: Development Profile: 121-room Artista Hotel approved by San Antonio design review commission
item.description: [Texas & San Antonio Local] Keyword: San Antonio — The 121-room Artista Hotel planned for a compact property on East Travis Street and the river received final approval from the Historic and Design Review Commission on Wednesday. The post Development Profile: 121-room Artista Hotel approved by San Antonio design review commission appeared first on San Antonio Heron.

item.title: UTSA receives funding for next Dolorosa building in downtown San Antonio
item.description: [Texas & San Antonio Local] Keyword: San Antonio — UTSA's $124 million Innovation, Entrepreneurship and Careers (IEC) building, the university's next phase on Dolorosa, received full funding. The post UTSA receives funding for next Dolorosa building in downtown San Antonio appeared first on San Antonio Heron.

item.title: Houston developer Urban Genesis prepares to start 3 apartment projects in Tobin Hill
item.description: [Texas & San Antonio Local] Keyword: San Antonio — Houston developer Urban Genesis says it’s on schedule to start construction on its holdings on and off North St. Mary’s Street—overall, a $45 million series of developments for a total of 178 market-rate apartments—by first quarter 2023. The post Houston developer Urban Genesis prepares to start 3 apartment projects in Tobin Hill appeared first on San Antonio Heron.

item.title: Alazan Courts, Cattleman Square Lofts among 14 projects set to receive $44M in San Antonio bond funding
item.description: [Texas & San Antonio Local] Keyword: San Antonio — The city of San Antonio is preparing to disburse nearly $44 million to housing developers and landlords for the production of 686 apartments, the repair of another 1,775 units, and the construction of 71 single-family homes. The post Alazan Courts, Cattleman Square Lofts among 14 projects set to receive $44M in San Antonio bond funding appeared first on San Antonio Heron.

item.title: Freetail Brewing Co. beer tasting to benefit Heron journalism
item.description: [Texas & San Antonio Local] Keyword: San Antonio — The San Antonio Heron and Freetail Brewing Co. are teaming up for a beer tasting event this Thursday in support of local journalism. The post Freetail Brewing Co. beer tasting to benefit Heron journalism appeared first on San Antonio Heron.

item.title: Editor Ben Olivo leaving the Heron
item.description: [Texas & San Antonio Local] Keyword: San Antonio — The Heron will go on hiatus beginning Jan. 1, so the organization can regroup. The post Editor Ben Olivo leaving the Heron appeared first on San Antonio Heron.

item.title: GrayStreet sells key Southtown retail strip to Austin firm
item.description: [Texas & San Antonio Local] Keyword: San Antonio — A popular Southtown retail strip, home to Pharm Table, Mixtli and Brown Coffee Co., has been sold to an Austin firm. The post GrayStreet sells key Southtown retail strip to Austin firm appeared first on San Antonio Heron.

item.title: Out of Reach: Why San Antonio can’t get a grip on its affordable housing crisis
item.description: [Texas & San Antonio Local] Keyword: San Antonio — With construction costs and interest rates soaring, San Antonio developers are struggling to make the numbers work for their affordable housing projects. The post Out of Reach: Why San Antonio can’t get a grip on its affordable housing crisis appeared first on San Antonio Heron.

item.title: San Antonio Heron has shut down
item.description: [Texas & San Antonio Local] Keyword: San Antonio — We have a final special announcement: It is with heartfelt sadness that we announce that the Heron has ceased operations. The post San Antonio Heron has shut down appeared first on San Antonio Heron.

item.title: A Central Texas Library Closing the Digital Divide
item.description: [Texas & San Antonio Local] Keyword: San Antonio — In Caldwell County, Texas, between Austin and San Antonio, some residents are still struggling to keep up in an increasingly digital world. With broadband access lagging behind neighboring metro counties, older residents in particular are feeling the strain. Now, the Martindale Community Library is stepping in to help close that gap. Census estimates from 2024 […] The post A Central Texas Library Closing the Digital Divide appeared first on The Daily Yonder.

item.title: [$] Fedora: 2FA, or not 2FA, that is the question
item.description: [AI & Automation] Keyword: AI agent — Compromised accounts are one of the most common ways that attackers can sneak malware into the open-source supply chain. One way to reduce account compromise is for projects to require two-factor authentication (2FA) or multi-factor authentication (MFA), but that is easier said than done. However, Fedora is currently discussing putting 2FA requirements in place soon, following an an alleged account compromise that led to an AI agent causing a number of problems for the project. After some discus

item.title: Podcast: If AI Is Sentient Then So Is 'Age of Empires II'
item.description: [AI & Automation] Keyword: LLM — A surreal but compelling LLM experiment with Age of Empires II; how a Texas city sold land meant for a park to a data center company; and the Madison Square Garden hack.

item.title: How Californians can use a new state website to block hundreds of data brokers
item.description: [Digital Rights & Privacy] Keyword: data broker — A tool called DROP lets California residents fill out a few forms to keep their personal data from being tracked or sold by data brokers.

item.title: Following Markup investigation, Congress finds data brokers cost consumers tens of billions of dollars
item.description: [Digital Rights & Privacy] Keyword: data broker — A congressional investigation estimates broker breaches have cost consumers $20 billion in identity theft. Major brokers now promise to make it easier to opt out of their databases.

item.title: It’s easier for Californians to escape data brokers following a Markup investigation
item.description: [Digital Rights & Privacy] Keyword: data broker — The Markup and CalMatters showed how website code could make it harder for Californians to exercise their right to remove personal data. Now much of that code has disappeared.

item.title: The form asked my permission to share my health data. Then it wouldn’t let me say no.
item.description: [Cannabis, Hemp & Drug Policy] Keyword: THCA — Dark patterns force patients to share their data with big healthcare networks, even when the privacy form they’re signing explicitly says they can opt-out.

item.title: He Almost Died From A Burst Appendix. A For-Profit Jail Medical Provider Dismissed It As Heartburn.
item.description: [Cannabis, Hemp & Drug Policy] Keyword: THCA — A new lawsuit in York County, Pennsylvania, highlights a pattern of allegations of medical neglect by for-profit correctional healthcare company PrimeCare. The post He Almost Died From A Burst Appendix. A For-Profit Jail Medical Provider Dismissed It As Heartburn. appeared first on The Appeal.

item.title: Indigenous cultural practices are a climate solution, report finds
item.description: [Indigenous News & Tribal Sovereignty] Keyword: Indigenous —

item.title: The Trees Are Speaking
item.description: [Indigenous News & Tribal Sovereignty] Keyword: Indigenous — Indigenous scientists are hurrying to learn what they can about intact old-growth forests before they are gone.

item.title: The Fight to Repatriate Indigenous Students Who Died at Boarding Schools
item.description: [Indigenous News & Tribal Sovereignty] Keyword: Indigenous — On March 31, the Department of Government Efficiency announced cuts of $1.6 million to projects designed to preserve the stories of federal Indian boarding school victims and provide healing to

item.title: Indigenous Stewards Reclaim Prison Land
item.description: [Indigenous News & Tribal Sovereignty] Keyword: Indigenous — The Appalachian Rekindling Project purchased land in Kentucky that was designated for a federal prison.

item.title: How Standing Rock Changed Us All
item.description: [Indigenous News & Tribal Sovereignty] Keyword: Indigenous — Ten years ago this spring, an Indigenous-led movement against an oil pipeline transformed the consciousness of the country.

item.title: Alaska GOP Pushes 800-Mile Gas Terminal and Pipeline That Could Decimate Climate
item.description: [Indigenous News & Tribal Sovereignty] Keyword: Indigenous — Republicans in Alaska want tax breaks for a Trump-backed megaproject that Indigenous activists oppose.

item.title: New GEF Project Raises Hope for Change in India’s Indigenous Lake Community
item.description: [Indigenous News & Tribal Sovereignty] Keyword: Indigenous — At dawn, when the waters of Dumboor Lake lie still under a pale grey sky, Santo Chakma, 63, nudges his narrow wooden boat into a reservoir that swallowed his childhood. The lake is a growing attraction for tourists who come here in search of beauty and tranquillity, with dozens of islands scattered across a vast […]

item.title: Colombia’s next President: A Reckoning for Peace, Climate and Human Rights
item.description: [Indigenous News & Tribal Sovereignty] Keyword: Indigenous — On 21 June Colombians made their choice. By the narrowest of margins, Abelardo de la Espriella, a far-right criminal lawyer who’s never held elected office, became president-elect. Climate activists, human rights defenders, Indigenous communities and peace advocates have the most to lose from the incoming government’s agenda. The election results follow the logic of a […]

item.title: Trump-shuttered climate change site back online in nonprofit hands
item.description: [Climate & Environment] Keyword: climate change — Remove something from the internet? You can't stop the (climate change) signal, Mal

item.title: ZTE builds a TCO-optimal AI factory to fuel token economy
item.description: [Worker Cooperatives & Solidarity Economy] Keyword: co-op — PARTNER CONTENT: Leveraging OEX architecture SuperPODs and multi-dimensional co-design to maximize tokens per second and lower total cost of ownership for scaled inference

item.title: Collabora releases CODE 26.04 as rivalry between FOSS cloudy office suites heats up
item.description: [Open Source Software] Keyword: FOSS — Now with Markdown support and smarter formula error handling – plus integrated AI, though it's off by default

item.title: FOSS dev builds a BASIC compiler using LLVM
item.description: [Open Source Software] Keyword: FOSS — Not just any old BASIC, either: OS-9’s BASIC09

item.title: Three FOSS projects for developers, procrastinators, and media wranglers
item.description: [Open Source Software] Keyword: FOSS — Prism tracks AI emissions, Super Productivity keeps tasks local, and TAMOSS brings the BBC's media API to Kubernetes

item.title: Oracle promises to open up MySQL governance, but the community wants guarantees
item.description: [Open Source Software] Keyword: open source — Open source advocates remain concerned over lack of binding commitments

item.title: Is there anything GLP-1 agonist drugs cannot do?
item.description: [Diabetes & Kidney Disease] Keyword: GLP-1 — Every other month, researchers find new uses for GLP-1 agonist drugs, like Mounjaro and Ozempic — now even as a treatment for substance abuse. It's classic drug repurposing, a technique that saved lives during COVID.

item.title: One common fat may fuel type 2 diabetes while another helps fight it
item.description: [Diabetes & Kidney Disease] Keyword: type 2 diabetes — Not all fats affect your body the same way. Researchers found that palmitic acid, a saturated fat common in many foods, may contribute to insulin resistance and type 2 diabetes by triggering inflammation, toxic fat buildup, and cellular stress. In contrast, oleic acid—the heart-healthy fat abundant in olive oil—appears to help protect insulin function and may even counter some of the harmful effects of palmitic acid.

item.title: Super-Potent Synthetic Opioids Spread Across US Amid Fentanyl Crackdown
item.description: [Opioid Treatment & Methadone] Keyword: fentanyl — This article was co-published with Signal Ohio and STAT. In high school, Ashley Delgado dreamed of becoming a doctor and one day buying her father a Rolls-Royce. “She wanted to heal people,” said her father, James Taylor. She had a high GPA, Taylor added, and did especially well in science and Latin. In her mid-20s, […] The post Super-Potent Synthetic Opioids Spread Across US Amid Fentanyl Crackdown appeared first on bellingcat.

item.title: A War Zone, Minus the War: One Year Later, Has the Military Really Secured the US-Mexico Border?
item.description: [Immigration & Border] Keyword: border wall — On a warm, winter Sunday, the Playas de Tijuana in Mexico is filled with families picnicking. The beach here presses right up against the border wall with the United States. Music blares, teenagers film TikTok videos next to the 30-foot high fence, which is covered in painted murals on the Mexican side—butterflies, faces, human hands […] The post A War Zone, Minus the War: One Year Later, Has the Military Really Secured the US-Mexico Border? appeared first on The War Horse.

item.title: Ethiopia’s Ballot Box Must Become a Symbol for Peace
item.description: [Housing & Gentrification] Keyword: displacement — In the dust and uncertainty of contemporary Ethiopia, one image from the June 2026 election lingers with unusual force: Millions of citizens stand patiently in lines to vote while vast parts of the country remain scarred by war, displacement and grief. More than 50 million Ethiopians registered to vote, with turnout reportedly approaching 90% in… Continue reading Ethiopia’s Ballot Box Must Become a Symbol for Peace The post Ethiopia’s Ballot Box Must Become a Symbol for Peace appeared firs

item.title: FIFA World Cup 2026: A Reminder That Sports Are Also an Educational Tool
item.description: [Housing & Gentrification] Keyword: displacement — In many parts of the world, children arrive at school carrying far more than books. They carry displacement, exclusion, trauma and the quiet weight of inequality. Education systems often respond with new strategies, revised standards and another round of teacher training. Far less often do we consider the role that movement and physical activity can… Continue reading FIFA World Cup 2026: A Reminder That Sports Are Also an Educational Tool The post FIFA World Cup 2026: A Reminder That Sport

item.title: Inside the scramble to stop the displacement of thousands of California foster children
item.description: [Housing & Gentrification] Keyword: displacement — For years, the Nonprofits Insurance Alliance of California had been the primary insurer for the state’s foster family agencies, which house some of the most vulnerable children, including many LGBTQ+  youth, those with severe behavioral or medical needs or who have previously suffered abuse. But in August, NIAC announced it would no longer insure these […] The post Inside the scramble to stop the displacement of thousands of California foster children appeared first on Center for Publ

item.title: Notion kills its Gmail client after AI agents keep humans from troubling inbox
item.description: [AI & Automation] Keyword: AI agent — More than half of users now let bots handle email, so service is headed for shutdown

item.title: Ex-Huntress analyst claims company insider fed info to a ransomware crim. Social media drama ensues
item.description: [Cybersecurity] Keyword: ransomware — Former employee accuses company of prioritizing pending IPO over client security

item.title: Self-destructing Mistic backdoor linked to access broker selling corporate footholds to ransomware gangs
item.description: [Cybersecurity] Keyword: ransomware — Spotted in intrusions targeting insurance, education, IT, and professional services sectors

item.title: Bananas could vanish from US school meals. Here’s why
item.description: [USDA & Farm Grants] Keyword: farm bill — New Farm Bill places caps on non-US foods; nutritionists say it restricts availability of healthy meals for kidsSchool nutrition workers and advocates have “lots of concerns about bananas”, said Erin Ogden, policy associate for federal child nutrition programs at the Center for Science in the Public Interest (CSPI).Bananas are nutrient-dense foods that many children like. That makes them popular offerings in school cafeterias, since any healthy food that a kid will eat prevents waste and ensures
